notes.introduction: A verse-by-verse exposition of Genesis 12:1-9, tracing Abram's departure from Haran to Canaan, the instruction God gave him, the promises of the Abrahamic Covenant, and the record of his obedience and worship along the journey.
outline: I. The Instruction Received: God's call to Abram to leave for a land He would show him (v. 1), with reference to Acts 7:1-4 and the earlier call in Ur

II. The Promises Rehearsed: The unconditional 'I will' promises of the Abrahamic Covenant covering nationhood, blessing, a great name, and blessing to all families of the earth through the seed (vv. 2-3), traced through Genesis 22:18 and Galatians 3

III. Abram's Obedience Recorded: The departure from Haran, the journey to Canaan, the stops at Shechem and Bethel, the Lord's appearance and land promise, Abram's altar-building and worship, and his continued travel southward (vv. 4-9), with concluding reference to Hebrews 11:8-10
